metadata_policy.description_strategy: Codex-only, trigger-first summaries; Claude descriptions remain unchanged

project-only.summary: Install no global marketplace skills; rely on the current project's .agents/skills mirror.

build.summary: Code navigation, convergence, execution, and session orientation.

all.summary: Every generated skill; use only when the full roster is needed.

capabilities.summary: scripts/lib/codex-compat.mjs#lowerInteractiveChoiceProse replaces exact AskUserQuestion prose constructs with host-neutral chooser terminology in every compiled profile. #injectInteractiveChoiceContract inserts the canonical executable contract for all nine choice consumers: use request_user_input when callable; otherwise ask one concise direct question and end the turn; never execute before an explicit choice; preserve source-owned choices, recommendations, opt-outs, one-shot behavior, and explicit-invocation semantics. Ratcheted to zero across all generated output (codex-compatibility Phase 3).

scripts/lib/codex-compat.mjs#lowerWorkerProse replaces the exact Agent-call / subagent_type=Explore / subagent_type=general-purpose / hyphenated sub-agent(s) constructs with Codex worker vocabulary (read-only explorer worker, scoped executor worker). Ratcheted to zero across all generated output (codex-compatibility Phase 1). Phase 2 adds the concrete supervision/dispatch machinery on top: scripts/lib/codex-compat.mjs#injectWorkerDispatchContract appends a 'Worker dispatch contract (Codex)' section — the canonical work-packet contract (#CODEX_WORK_PACKET_CONTRACT: goal / scope+owned files / inputs+source of truth / constraints / done_when / verification commands / base SHA / return schema) and worker return contract (#CODEX_WORKER_RETURN_CONTRACT: status done|partial|blocked / files changed / diff summary / commands+results / assumptions / unresolved risks / current SHA) — to the five dispatch-heavy skills (nav-audit, nav-plan, nav-refactor, research-dissect, shape-build), each referencing that skill's own existing inject/check prose rather than duplicating it.

scripts/lib/codex-compat.mjs#lowerFrontmatter strips disable-model-invocation from Codex frontmatter and injects an explicit body-level invocation-policy paragraph near the top of the skill. Ratcheted to zero across all generated output (codex-compatibility Phase 1).

scripts/lib/codex-compat.mjs#lowerFrontmatter strips model: sonnet from Codex frontmatter and injects an execution-tier note (delegate to the configured executor role where available, else run inline and report the degradation); lowerWorkerProse also rewrites remaining body-prose mentions of the removed field. Ratcheted to zero across all generated output (codex-compatibility Phase 1).

scripts/lib/codex-compat.mjs#lowerBrowserVerifyProse lowers the exact Claude-only browser runtime ownership/path claims before contract injection, leaving generic browser-helper prose untouched. scripts/lib/codex-compat.mjs#injectBrowserVerifyContract inserts the canonical executable browser contract for the three shape consumers: dispatch to `.codex/agents/browser-verifier.toml` only when custom-agent runtime is available and that artifact exists; otherwise run the identical pass inline. Artifact/runtime absence is fallback, not `MISSING-TOOL`; preserve `PASS | DRIFT | BLOCKED | MISSING-TOOL`, screenshot paths only, and close-on-exit. scripts/build-codex.mjs#buildBrowserVerifierAgent compiles the source shape agent into the project runtime artifact. Legacy browser-subagent/source-path claims ratchet to zero; `agent-browser` remains a declared optional helper, not an unresolved token.

source_signals.pattern: ## browser-verify slot (the dependency, handled as a capability)
Driving the frontend uses shape's shared **browser-verify slot**

optional_dependencies.missing_behavior: If the selected override/default helper is missing, return `MISSING-TOOL` for the browser pass immediately; never auto-install from inside the pass. If custom-agent runtime or `.codex/agents/browser-verifier.toml` is unavailable, run the identical pass inline instead.

capabilities.summary: scripts/lib/codex-compat.mjs#createProjectGuidanceLowerer moves the existing CLAUDE.md->AGENTS.md + namespace-flatten rewrites here and adds /init-assumption softening (Claude Code's built-in /init -> generic architecture-enrichment phrasing, no Claude-only command claim). claude_built_in and claude_init categories ratcheted to zero (codex-compatibility Phase 1).
generated_contract_markers: architecture enrichment adds detail once code exists.
